2026-04-22T08:39:27.8273184Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8273759Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8274186Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8274553Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8274860Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8275098Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8275255Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8275433Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8275602Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8275831Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8276044Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8276338Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:39:27.8277013Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-22T08:40:37.3828618Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T08:40:19.328691692Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58490,\"title\":\"[http-client-csharp-mgmt] Public `(AzureLocation location)` ctor inconsistently emitted for hierarchy-built PATCH models\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen a TypeSpec model uses `@@hierarchyBuilding` to inherit from `TrackedResource` and represents a **PATCH** body (i.e., a sibling Update model), the Azure Management generator inconsistently emits the public `(AzureLocation location)` constructor that chains to `base(location)`. It is emitted for some patch models but not others, depending on whether the model\\u0026#39;s `properties` envelope qualifies for \\u0026#34;safe-flatten.\\u0026#34;\\n\\nThis makes Patch models with multi-property envelopes lose backward compatibility with the previous AutoRest-generated public ctor, forcing SDK consumers to write `[CodeGenSuppress]` partials that manually chain `: base(location)`.\\n\\n## Repro context\\n\\nFound while migrating `Azure.ResourceManager.NetApp` from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec (PR Azure/azure-sdk-for-net#55560). Four sibling Patch models with **identical TypeSpec structure** (same `@@hierarchyBuilding` to `TrackedResource`, same shape) produced different output:\\n\\n| Patch model | Envelope public-property count | Public `(AzureLocation location)` ctor emitted? |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `NetAppAccountPatch` | matches safe-flatten gate | ✅ yes |\\n| `CapacityPoolPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n| `NetAppBackupPolicyPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n| `SnapshotPolicyPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n\\nOld AutoRest (Swagger) emitted the public ctor for **all** of them via the hard-coded `TrackedResourceData` reference-class entry in `ReferenceClassFinder.cs:51-59`.\\n\\n## Root cause analysis\\n\\n1. **MTG `ModelProvider.BuildConstructors()`** ([ModelProvider.cs:600-604](https://github.com/microsoft/typespec/blob/main/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator/src/Providers/ModelProvider.cs#L600-L604)) always assigns `Public` accessibility to the init ctor of any `Input`-usage model. So MTG itself does not gate on \\u0026#34;model has a public derived property.\\u0026#34;\\n\\n2. **`InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor.Update()`** reparents the patch model to `TrackedResourceData` *after* MTG has already cached its constructors. The cached init ctor was built against the original spec base, which had no required `location`, so its parameter list does not include `location` and there is no `: base(location)` initializer.\\n\\n3. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor.UpdatePublicConstructor()`** ([FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:791-854](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L791-L854)) is the code that *would* repair the base initializer call. But it only runs when the model (or its base) was **safe-flattened**, and safe-flatten requires the inner `Properties` envelope to have **exactly 1** public property:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   // FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:608-612\\n   var publicPropertyCount = innerProperties.Count(p =\\u0026gt;\\n       p.Modifiers.HasFlag(MethodSignatureModifiers.Public) \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !IsObsoleteProperty(p));\\n   if (publicPropertyCount != 1)\\n   {\\n       continue;  // not safe-flattened → UpdatePublicConstructor never called\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n   Patch envelopes typically have many properties → they fall off this path → no public init ctor for the patch model, and no `: base(location)` chaining.\\n\\n## Minimum TypeSpec to reproduce\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/http\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/versioning\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Rest;\\nusing TypeSpec.Versioning;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ClientGenerator.Core;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace(\\u0026#34;Microsoft.Repro\\u0026#34;)\\n@service(#{ title: \\u0026#34;Repro\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Microsoft.Repro;\\n\\nenum Versions { @useDependency(Azure.Core.Versions.v1_0_Preview_2) v1: \\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\u0026#34; }\\n\\n// ─── Single-property envelope (works correctly) ────────────────────────────\\nmodel SingleProp is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;singleName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;single\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel SingleProps { onlyProp?: string; }\\nmodel SinglePropPatch {\\n  tags?: Record;\\n  location?: string;\\n  properties?: SinglePropsUpdate;\\n}\\nmodel SinglePropsUpdate { onlyProp?: string; }\\n\\n// ─── Multi-property envelope (broken) ────\\nmodel MultiProp is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;multiName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;multi\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel MultiProps { propA?: string; propB?: int32; propC?: boolean; }\\nmodel MultiPropPatch {\\n  tags?: Record;\\n  location?: string;\\n  properties?: MultiPropsUpdate;\\n}\\nmodel MultiPropsUpdate { propA?: string; propB?: int32; propC?: boolean; }\\n\\n// Re-parent both Patch models onto TrackedResource via hierarchy building\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(SinglePropPatch, SingleProp);\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(MultiPropPatch, MultiProp);\\n\\ninterface Operations extends Azure.ResourceManager.Operations {}\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface SingleOps {\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchSync;\\n}\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface MultiOps {\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchSync;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Expected output\\n\\nBoth `SinglePropPatch` and `MultiPropPatch` should generate:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic SinglePropPatch(AzureLocation location) : base(location) { }\\npublic MultiPropPatch(AzureLocation location) : base(location) { }\\n```\\n\\n### Actual output\\n\\n- `SinglePropPatch` → public ctor emitted with `: base(location)` ✅\\n- `MultiPropPatch` → only `internal MultiPropPatch(...)` full ctor emitted; no public init ctor ❌\\n\\n## Suggested fix\\n\\nThe \\u0026#34;safe-flatten\\u0026#34; gate in `FlattenPropertyVisitor` shouldn\\u0026#39;t be coupled with public-ctor base-initializer repair for hierarchy-built (PATCH-style) models. Two options:\\n\\n1. Decouple `UpdatePublicConstructorBaseInitializer` from the safe-flatten path. When `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor` reparents a model to a system base like `TrackedResourceData`, it should also rebuild/patch the model\\u0026#39;s public init ctor to include the base\\u0026#39;s required parameters and chain `: base(...)` accordingly.\\n\\n2. Have `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor` invalidate MTG\\u0026#39;s cached constructors so MTG\\u0026#39;s lazy `BuildConstructors()` re-runs against the new (system) base, naturally picking up `Location` as a required base property.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nSDK authors can add custom partial constructors that chain to `base(location)` manually — see [Azure/azure-sdk-for-net#55560 commit `56f55cc1508`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/55560/commits/56f55cc1508).\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58490\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T08:37:56Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-22T08:38:57Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-22T08:40:37.3855536Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T08:40:19.343837853Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58490,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-22T08:40:37.3887096Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T08:40:19.583490498Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58490,\"title\":\"[http-client-csharp-mgmt] Public `(AzureLocation location)` ctor inconsistently emitted for hierarchy-built PATCH models\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nWhen a TypeSpec model uses `@@hierarchyBuilding` to inherit from `TrackedResource` and represents a **PATCH** body (i.e., a sibling Update model), the Azure Management generator inconsistently emits the public `(AzureLocation location)` constructor that chains to `base(location)`. It is emitted for some patch models but not others, depending on whether the model\\u0026#39;s `properties` envelope qualifies for \\u0026#34;safe-flatten.\\u0026#34;\\n\\nThis makes Patch models with multi-property envelopes lose backward compatibility with the previous AutoRest-generated public ctor, forcing SDK consumers to write `[CodeGenSuppress]` partials that manually chain `: base(location)`.\\n\\n## Repro context\\n\\nFound while migrating `Azure.ResourceManager.NetApp` from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec (PR Azure/azure-sdk-for-net#55560). Four sibling Patch models with **identical TypeSpec structure** (same `@@hierarchyBuilding` to `TrackedResource`, same shape) produced different output:\\n\\n| Patch model | Envelope public-property count | Public `(AzureLocation location)` ctor emitted? |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `NetAppAccountPatch` | matches safe-flatten gate | ✅ yes |\\n| `CapacityPoolPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n| `NetAppBackupPolicyPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n| `SnapshotPolicyPatch` | multiple | ❌ no |\\n\\nOld AutoRest (Swagger) emitted the public ctor for **all** of them via the hard-coded `TrackedResourceData` reference-class entry in `ReferenceClassFinder.cs:51-59`.\\n\\n## Root cause analysis\\n\\n1. **MTG `ModelProvider.BuildConstructors()`** ([ModelProvider.cs:600-604](https://github.com/microsoft/typespec/blob/main/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator/src/Providers/ModelProvider.cs#L600-L604)) always assigns `Public` accessibility to the init ctor of any `Input`-usage model. So MTG itself does not gate on \\u0026#34;model has a public derived property.\\u0026#34;\\n\\n2. **`InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor.Update()`** reparents the patch model to `TrackedResourceData` *after* MTG has already cached its constructors. The cached init ctor was built against the original spec base, which had no required `location`, so its parameter list does not include `location` and there is no `: base(location)` initializer.\\n\\n3. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor.UpdatePublicConstructor()`** ([FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:791-854](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs#L791-L854)) is the code that *would* repair the base initializer call. But it only runs when the model (or its base) was **safe-flattened**, and safe-flatten requires the inner `Properties` envelope to have **exactly 1** public property:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   // FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs:608-612\\n   var publicPropertyCount = innerProperties.Count(p =\\u0026gt;\\n       p.Modifiers.HasFlag(MethodSignatureModifiers.Public) \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; !IsObsoleteProperty(p));\\n   if (publicPropertyCount != 1)\\n   {\\n       continue;  // not safe-flattened → UpdatePublicConstructor never called\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n   Patch envelopes typically have many properties → they fall off this path → no public init ctor for the patch model, and no `: base(location)` chaining.\\n\\n## Minimum TypeSpec to reproduce\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/rest\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/http\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@typespec/versioning\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Rest;\\nusing TypeSpec.Versioning;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ClientGenerator.Core;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace(\\u0026#34;Microsoft.Repro\\u0026#34;)\\n@service(#{ title: \\u0026#34;Repro\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Microsoft.Repro;\\n\\nenum Versions { @useDependency(Azure.Core.Versions.v1_0_Preview_2) v1: \\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\u0026#34; }\\n\\n// ─── Single-property envelope (works correctly) ────────────────────────────\\nmodel SingleProp is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;singleName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;single\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel SingleProps { onlyProp?: string; }\\nmodel SinglePropPatch {\\n  tags?: Record;\\n  location?: string;\\n  properties?: SinglePropsUpdate;\\n}\\nmodel SinglePropsUpdate { onlyProp?: string; }\\n\\n// ─── Multi-property envelope (broken) ────\\nmodel MultiProp is TrackedResource {\\n  @key(\\u0026#34;multiName\\u0026#34;) @segment(\\u0026#34;multi\\u0026#34;) @path name: string;\\n}\\nmodel MultiProps { propA?: string; propB?: int32; propC?: boolean; }\\nmodel MultiPropPatch {\\n  tags?: Record;\\n  location?: string;\\n  properties?: MultiPropsUpdate;\\n}\\nmodel MultiPropsUpdate { propA?: string; propB?: int32; propC?: boolean; }\\n\\n// Re-parent both Patch models onto TrackedResource via hierarchy building\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(SinglePropPatch, SingleProp);\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(MultiPropPatch, MultiProp);\\n\\ninterface Operations extends Azure.ResourceManager.Operations {}\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface SingleOps {\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchSync;\\n}\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface MultiOps {\\n  update is ArmCustomPatchSync;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Expected output\\n\\nBoth `SinglePropPatch` and `MultiPropPatch` should generate:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic SinglePropPatch(AzureLocation location) : base(location) { }\\npublic MultiPropPatch(AzureLocation location) : base(location) { }\\n```\\n\\n### Actual output\\n\\n- `SinglePropPatch` → public ctor emitted with `: base(location)` ✅\\n- `MultiPropPatch` → only `internal MultiPropPatch(...)` full ctor emitted; no public init ctor ❌\\n\\n## Suggested fix\\n\\nThe \\u0026#34;safe-flatten\\u0026#34; gate in `FlattenPropertyVisitor` shouldn\\u0026#39;t be coupled with public-ctor base-initializer repair for hierarchy-built (PATCH-style) models. Two options:\\n\\n1. Decouple `UpdatePublicConstructorBaseInitializer` from the safe-flatten path. When `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor` reparents a model to a system base like `TrackedResourceData`, it should also rebuild/patch the model\\u0026#39;s public init ctor to include the base\\u0026#39;s required parameters and chain `: base(...)` accordingly.\\n\\n2. Have `InheritableSystemObjectModelVisitor` invalidate MTG\\u0026#39;s cached constructors so MTG\\u0026#39;s lazy `BuildConstructors()` re-runs against the new (system) base, naturally picking up `Location` as a required base property.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nSDK authors can add custom partial constructors that chain to `base(location)` manually — see [Azure/azure-sdk-for-net#55560 commit `56f55cc1508`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/55560/commits/56f55cc1508).\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58490\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T08:37:56Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-22T08:38:57Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
